title: Skill inputs.json metaschema
description: Validates per-skill inputs.json files. Three top-level conventions are accepted: items[] (the canonical WI1 interview taxonomy, used by db-layout / earthing / fault-level / lighting-layout / sld — each item validated against InputItem), inputs[] (legacy form used by arc-flash + arc-flash-labelling), input_groups[] (grouped form used by cable-sizing + small-power). Only items[] currently carries strict per-item validation.

skill.description: Skill id matching the parent skill.manifest.json

items.description: Canonical WI1 interview items. Used by db-layout, earthing, fault-level, lighting-layout, sld.

inputs.description: Legacy alias used by arc-flash + arc-flash-labelling. Item shape is not currently strict-validated.

input_groups.description: Grouped form used by cable-sizing + small-power. Item shape is not currently strict-validated.

label.description: Human-readable question text shown to the engineer

hint.description: Optional clarifying text shown under the question

answer_type.description: Determines UI control and validator behaviour

options.description: Required when answer_type is enum or enum_list

default.description: Optional default value; shape must match answer_type

required.description: true = engineer must answer before skill runs

validator.description: Reference to a named validator in shared/validation/validators.json

depends_on.description: IDs of items whose answers must be present before this question is shown

project_fact_candidate.description: Forward-compat: items describing the building (not THIS room/design) that a future project-memory feature can pre-fill

item_schema.description: Required when answer_type=struct_list; JSON Schema for each list element. Depth 1 only — no nested struct_list.
